reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed questioned the necessity of moving metadata out of the swap table, suggesting that reusing it as the representation of virtual swap space could be more efficient and concise.
reviews.raw_body:
I didn't look through the entire series, but let me ask the same
high-level question I asked before. Instead of moving things out of the
swap table, why not reuse the swap table as the representation of the
virtual swap space? Seems like most/all metadata is already moved there
in a nice concise format.

2026-05-11.patch_summary: This patch series, based on Linux kernel version 6.19, aims to improve performance and reduce overhead in virtual swap space management. The changes focus on reducing CPU and memory usage on fast swap backends such as zram and PMEM. Key improvements include combining multiple vswap operations, batching per-entry operations, eliminating redundant lookups, and optimizing the reverse map to minimize physical-to-virtual swap indirection costs.
